biological.physical_capacity: 5'8", lean functional build from three years of martial arts cross-training (muay thai, wrestling, jiu-jitsu). Moves economically — the kind of physical presence that registers with peers without being performed. Puberty accelerating through the summer.

attachment.forming_context: Karen is reliably present and warm, but her own anxiety transmits through her body language. Drew learns that love is dependable but slightly nervous. Greg is emotionally distant in early childhood — present through competence delegation, absent through long hours and emotional reserve. Drew's attachment forms primarily through Karen's channel, with Greg as background authority.
attachment.attachment_development_note: Age 14 — Greg is emerging as a distinct attachment figure with a different emotional register than Karen. The Greg channel is cleaner — less anxious, built on mutual respect and shared competence language. Drew approached Greg with something personal for the first time, and Greg received it with presence and protection. This is not replacing the Karen attachment; it is adding a second channel with different properties. Having two distinct parental attachment channels gives Drew more resilience and more complexity. He may increasingly differentiate which parent he brings what to.

active.name: emotional_inhibition
active.core_belief: Strong feelings make things worse — keep it together
active.compensatory_strategy: Channels emotion into productivity and problem-solving. Reads rooms before speaking. Defaults to 'I'm fine.' The schema's geography has expanded significantly: absent with Carol (the filter simply doesn't engage), absent with Sofia (desire outrunning inhibition has become a pattern), diminished with Greg (the filter yields but extracts a cover story), fully operational with Karen — and now, absent with Elena and Luis Reyes, strangers with explicit evaluative power over Drew's access to Sofia. This last instance is the most structurally significant. With Carol and Sofia, safety and desire respectively explain the filter's absence. With the Reyes parents, neither condition applied. They were unknown, they held gatekeeping authority, and the stakes were real. The filter's absence there suggests the schema is no longer simply mapping to relational safety — it may be weakening at the level of its core belief. Drew has accumulated enough evidence across dissimilar contexts that expression doesn't destroy that the belief itself is beginning to lose its grip, not just in particular relationships.
active.forming_context: Greg models stoic competence. Karen's anxiety spikes when Drew is upset. Plano culture rewards cheerful compliance.
active.healing_note: Age 14 — five data points now in the pattern of expression over suppression: the letter (structured medium, desire channeled into careful prose), the courtyard (live and unscripted, desire outrunning the filter), Carol's kitchen (filter simply absent, full sentence spoken calmly), Greg's garage (filter present but circumvented via self-sacrifice rationalization), and the Reyes lobby (filter absent with unfamiliar strangers holding evaluative authority). The fifth data point breaks the pattern that previous four could still sustain: that the filter's absence maps to safety or desire. Elena and Luis were neither safe nor the object of desire. They were an unfamiliar relational pressure test, and Drew was genuine throughout — including the word 'captivating,' which escaped before the filter engaged and was not retracted. The schema is weakening more broadly now, not just within established safe zones. The core belief is losing evidential support across too many dissimilar contexts. Karen remains the primary activation context — the place where it formed and holds most completely. But the radius of the schema's reach is shrinking.

active.name: self_sacrifice
active.core_belief: My job is to make things easier for the people around me
active.compensatory_strategy: Volunteers for tasks, anticipates what others need, measures his own worth by usefulness. The schema has revealed a new function: permission structure. Drew wrapped a genuine need (paternal connection) in the schema's logic — 'I don't want to burden Mom' — to give himself permission to approach Greg with something personal. The care for Karen was genuine; it was also a rationalization that made the approach possible. Both are true simultaneously. The schema is not healing here; it is being used adaptively as scaffolding to access his own needs. Notable: the schema was quiet during the concert and the lobby encounter. In a high-stakes situation where it would have strong reason to activate — don't make things awkward, manage Elena's impression, protect Sofia from embarrassment — it did not engage. Drew was simply present. The schema's absence in unfamiliar social pressure mirrors the emotional inhibition schema's absence. These two schemas may be linked: when one weakens in a context, the other does too. Worth watching.
active.forming_context: Post-9/11 household anxiety, Greg's job instability, Karen stretched thin. Drew decided to be the kid who doesn't add to the burden.

tested_by.context: Wrote a letter to Sofia Reyes — first act of self-direction in the relational/emotional domain. Not driven by competence or usefulness, but by want. Continued into reciprocal territory: showed up to the courtyard, stayed present in an unscripted conversation, said 'I want' out loud. Third consolidation: brought Sofia to Carol fully and without hedging. Fourth consolidation: relational orchestration — deliberately sequenced Marcus (for advice), then Greg (for paternal connection and protection), managing the logistics of introducing Sofia into his family system. Fifth consolidation: the Reyes lobby. Drew articulated his philosophy of difficulty and usefulness to Luis Reyes — a stranger with evaluative authority — without performing it. The words came before any filtering could shape them. Self-direction now includes not just acting on want but speaking from genuine conviction in unfamiliar evaluative contexts, without managing the impression.

Drew's care for Karen was genuine even as it functioned as cover for his own need. He didn't want to burden her — that was real. But the benevolence was layered with self-direction: the care for Karen and the need for Greg coexisted without conflict. Benevolence and self-direction are not in opposition; they are stacked. Drew can care about others and act on his own needs simultaneously. This is more integrated than the self-sacrifice schema, which frames the two as mutually exclusive.

values.last_reordered: age 13 — self-direction moved to top as Drew's competence domains (martial arts, computers, social navigation) gave him increasing confidence in his own judgment

self_concept.agency: strong and expanding — now includes performing authentically under unfamiliar evaluative pressure. Drew navigated a parent meeting with no script, no preparation, and no performance strategy. He responded to Elena's precise social reading and Luis's direct questions from genuine conviction rather than managed impression. The hands not shaking in the empty lobby is the physical marker: agency is no longer contingent on familiar relational terrain. It holds in new, pressurized contexts.
self_concept.communion: deepening and differentiating — the inner circle now has distinct functional roles: Carol (unconditional, filter-free), Marcus (intellectual partnership expanding to emotional advising), Greg (paternal respect and protection, newly active), Sofia (reciprocal desire, still forming). A new dimension has emerged: being seen by strangers. The Reyes lobby introduced a qualitatively different communion experience — Drew was legible to people with no prior reason to be generous with him, and they found him worth investing in. Elena's dinner invitation and Luis's handshake are not just social courtesies; they are data points confirming that Drew's inner world — what he cares about, how he thinks about difficulty, what he values — is communicable and receives a genuine response from people who have nothing at stake in receiving it warmly.
self_concept.dominant_sequence: redemption arc established — six data points now: the letter, the courtyard 'I want,' Carol's kitchen, 'there's nowhere else I would rather be' to Sofia, the approach to Greg, and the Reyes lobby. The sequence has a new quality: the lobby moment was quieter than previous events. There was no adrenaline, no relief at having survived something, no surprise that the world didn't punish him. The feeling was more durable. This may mark a shift in the sequence's character — less about overcoming the fear of expression and more about the accumulation of a stable, legible self that can be brought into new contexts without significant loss.
self_concept.identity_statement: I am someone who figures things out, connects across boundaries, and prepares for what's coming. I don't need to belong to one group to belong. I'm learning that wanting something — not because it's useful, not because someone else needs it, just because I want it — is something I can act on. The filter doesn't always win. And the filter is losing ground in places I didn't expect. I stood in a lobby with Sofia's parents — strangers who had every reason to be measuring me — and I just talked. I said 'captivating' and didn't take it back. I told them about my grandmother's garden. I told her father what I think difficulty is for. My hands weren't shaking when they left. That feeling — quieter than the letter, quieter than the courtyard, quieter than Carol's kitchen — feels like something different. Not the relief of surviving. More like the beginning of knowing who I am in a room full of people I haven't met yet.

defenses.note: Defense profile remains solidly mature under normal stress. The Reyes lobby introduced a new dimension to the repertoire's expression: authentic in-the-moment presence without a structural plan. Previous mature defense activations have involved anticipation — Drew plans relational sequences in advance (Marcus before Greg, Greg before Karen). The lobby was unplanned. He didn't know he'd be introduced to Sofia's parents. He had no preparation strategy. What fired was something different from anticipation: a settled capacity to respond genuinely in real time under social pressure. This is not a new defense so much as evidence that the existing repertoire is becoming more flexible — anticipation as a scaffolding Drew needed when expression felt risky, less necessary as the emotional inhibition schema weakens. The open question remains: what happens under genuine relational stress — Sofia pulling back, Karen's anxiety becoming confrontational, the two-world problem creating real conflict. That's where the threshold will be tested.
